name: Ruqayya bint Hussain
title: The Beloved of Karbala
shortDescription: Imam Hussain's (AS) three-year-old daughter who, after enduring the sufferings of captivity, passed away in the ruins of Sham upon seeing her father's severed head.
fullStory:
Ruqayya bint Hussain (AS) was the young and beloved daughter of the Master of Martyrs, who was only three or four years old during the event of Ashura. She had a deep affection and strong attachment to her noble father.

After Ashura, this young child was taken captive along with the rest of the caravan. On the long route to Kufa and Sham, she endured immense suffering, including thirst, exhaustion, the enemy's whips, and seeing severed heads on spears.

In the ruins of Sham, Ruqayya, who deeply missed her father, woke up crying one night asking for him. Her heart-wrenching cries took sleep away from the captives and even the Syrian guards.

Yazid ordered Imam Hussain's (AS) severed head to be taken to her. When Ruqayya embraced her father's bloody head, she confided in him, saying: 'Father! Who orphaned me at this young age?' She cried so much until she unjustly passed away embracing her father's head, becoming the eternal symbol of Ashura's oppression.
quoteArabic: يا أَبَتاهُ! مَنْ ذَا الَّذِي خَضَبَكَ بِدِمائِكَ؟...
quote: (While embracing her father's head:) Father! Who dyed your face with your blood? Father! Who orphaned me at this young age?
